member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView
member.summary: A base class for all views that can display geographic content.

remarks.para: Subclasses can display the content in either 2D () or 3D (). In an MVC architecture, this class and its subclasses represent the View tier. The Model tier is represented by , which supplies the geographic content as a map () or a scene ().

contains all the common operations and events that apply to displaying and working with maps and scenes. For example, you can change the viewable area by setting a , work with graphics in the , and identify a that displays in the view.

member.summary: Checks if DirectX software rendering is required.
member.remarks: This method checks the ArcGISEnvironment internal flag and system defined settings that force DirectX software rendering. This method is called anytime a new GeoView is loaded (a new dx device is created).
member.returns: true if software rendering is required; otherwise false

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.GraphicsOverlays
member.summary: Gets or sets a collection of objects used to display graphics on top of the view's content.

remarks.para: To display graphics in the , add the graphics to a and add the to this collection. The order of the objects in the collection reflects their display order in the view. The first in the collection is drawn beneath all other graphic overlays.
You cannot add duplicate objects to the collection or use this graphics overlay collection in another .
Use methods such as , to identify graphics in this collection.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.IdentifyGraphicsOverlayAsync(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.GraphicsOverlay,System.Windows.Point,System.Double,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Identifies the topmost graphic in the specified graphics overlay, at the given screen point.

remarks.para: As locations from user gestures are not always accurate to the exact pixel, you can define a tolerance for the identify operation. The tolerance parameter sets the radius of a circle, centered at the specified coordinates, in device-independent pixels (DIP). If the tolerance value is 0, identify performs the test at the specified coordinates. If it is greater than 0, identify tests inside the circle. For touch displays a value of 22 is recommended to cover an average finger tap. The maximum allowed value is 100 DIPs.

The parameter controls which properties are populated in the instance. If is true, is populated. If the overlay does not have popups an error is returned. If is false, and are populated, if the overlay has popups.

member.param: The graphics overlay in which to identify the graphic.
The screen coordinates to identify the graphic.
A radius in device-independent pixels (DIP) that specifies how precise the identify operation should be.
true includes only popups in the identify results. An error is returned if the layer does not have popups. false includes both geoelements and popups in the identify results.

remarks.para: As locations from user gestures are not always accurate to the exact pixel, you can define a tolerance for the identify operation. The tolerance parameter sets the radius of a circle, centered at the specified coordinates, in device-independent pixels (DIP). If the tolerance value is 0, identify performs the test at the specified coordinates. If it is greater than 0, identify tests inside the circle. For touch displays a value of 22 is recommended to cover an average finger tap. The maximum allowed value is 100 DIPs.

The result is returned in a single instance. If the parameter is true only is populated. If the layer does not have popups an error is returned. If the parameter is false, both and are populated, if the layer has popups. and provide the results in a top-to-bottom order.

member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous identify operation on the specified graphics overlay. The value of the TResult parameter contains a read-only collection of

The screen coordinates to identify the geoelements.
A radius in device-independent pixels (DIP) that specifies how precise the identify operation should be.
true includes only popups in the identify results. An error is returned if the layer does not have popups. false includes both geoelements and popups in the identify results.
The maximum number of geoelements and/or popups returned in the . A value of -1 indicates unlimited results.

remarks.para: As locations from user gestures are not always accurate to the exact pixel, you can define a tolerance for the identify operation. The tolerance parameter sets the radius of a circle, centered at the specified coordinates, in device-independent pixels (DIP). If the tolerance value is 0, identify performs the test at the specified coordinates. If it is greater than 0, identify tests inside the circle. For touch displays a value of 22 is recommended to cover an average finger tap. The maximum allowed value is 100 DIPs.

The result is returned in a single instance. If the parameter is true only is populated. If the layer does not have popups an error is returned. If the parameter is false, both and are populated, if the layer has popups. and provide the results in a top-to-bottom order.

member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous identify operation on the specified layer. The value of the TResult parameter contains

member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating current gesture in to ensure that once a drag gesture has started, it cannot change meaning.
member.remarks: For instance if drag starts with shift pressed, a zoom-box operation will start. If the user lets go of shift while dragging, the active manipulation will continue until . This is to avoid various drag gestures don't overlap, like for instance GeometryEditor interactions.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.SpatialReference
member.summary: Gets the spatial reference that defines the coordinate system used by the map or scene in the .

remarks.para: You can observe changes to this property by listening to the event.
The is used to relate map coordinates to locations in the real world. This property is available when the view's map or scene has been loaded.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.IsWrapAroundEnabled
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the wrap around feature is currently active. For wrap around to be active the property must be set to true and the SpatialReference of the MapView control must be a SpatialReference that supports wrapping.
member.value: true if continuous panning across the international date line is enabled in the , false otherwise.

remarks.para: By default, a attempts to wrap the or across the international date line for a continuous panning user experience. The eastern and western hemispheres wrap to form a continuous map, giving the impression that the map is endless.
Wraparound display is always enabled for a .
To disable wraparound behavior for a (or to reenable it), you can set the to the appropriate value. Wraparound can only be applied to a if certain conditions are met, as described in .

If wraparound is enabled, geometries returned from may have coordinates outside the domain of the spatial reference of the map. Before using such geometries to perform spatial queries, address finding, or as feature geometries in a geodatabase, you must normalize them to lie within the spatial reference domain using .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.IsNavigating
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the map is currently navigating. when navigation has finished the event will be raised.
member.value: true if the is animating a viewpoint change or a navigation gesture is in progress, otherwise false.
member.remarks: Indicates if the of a is currently changing. This can be due to a programmatic animated change to the , or user interaction with the view, such as panning and zooming.

member.name: E:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.NavigationCompleted
member.summary: When navigation on the map has completed this event will be raised.
member.remarks: New content is drawn every time a user pans or zooms the view, or the app programmatically changes its visible area. There is a short delay before this drawing phase completes. You can use to detect whether this drawing phase is in progress or has completed. For example, you may want to take a screen capture of a view's visible area () when drawing is completed. You can also use the event to accomplish the same thing.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.DrawStatus
member.summary: Gets the current drawing status of the content displayed in the .
member.remarks: New content is drawn every time a user pans or zooms the view, or the app programmatically changes its visible area. There is a short delay before this drawing phase completes. You can use to detect whether this drawing phase is in progress or has completed. For example, you may want to take a screen capture of a view's visible area () when drawing is completed. You can also use the event to accomplish the same thing.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.ViewInsets
member.summary: Gets or sets the viewport inset padding.

remarks.para: The reduces the "safe area" of the view, so zoom-to, center, rotation etc will be appropriately offset when panels are partially overlaying the view. For example if you pull out a side-panel that partially covers the map's left side and want to zoom to a geometry without the side-panel blocks the view of the panel, add some left-view padding equivalent to the width of the side-panel. Ex: mapView.ViewInsets = new Thickness(sidePanel.Width, 0, 0, 0);.
If you just want to zoom to a geometry and leave a bit of padding around it, use the method instead.
The ViewInsets setting currently only applies to , and is ignored on .

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.ViewInsetsProperty
member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.GetAttributionHeight
member.summary: Gets the height of the (collapsed) attribution area in DIPs.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.TimeExtent
member.summary: Gets or sets of the .
member.remarks: The defines how time-enabled data is displayed in the . Participating layers filter their data to only show data within the time extent set on the (see for details). The range of the time extent includes the start and end values. For example, a feature at 10/24/2016 would be included in a time extent defined as 10/24/2016 - 11/14/2016). Time filtering is not enabled until a non-null time extent is set on the . When the time extent is null on the, no time filtering is applied and all content is rendered.

member.name: F:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.TimeExtentProperty
member.summary: Identifies the dependency property.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.Overlays
member.summary: Gets or sets the XAML elements that can be used to overlay on the map, and placed using the ViewOverlayAnchor attached property.

remarks.para: The term 'overlay' in the ArcGIS Maps SDK for .NET may be new to some developers. In other Esri SDK's the concept of an overlay is often termed a 'MapTip'. A MapTip is a user-assistance component that displays an on-screen description of a map feature when the mouse is paused over or a users taps on a feature.

In order to maintain good performance in your application it is recommended to not place too many or overly complex UIElement objects within the UIElementCollection. These graphical UIElement objects are drawn via software rendering of the Operating System and performance can vary across devices. Keeping UIElements to a minimum, say under 20, should not impact the performance of your application when setting the MapView.Overlays Property.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.Grid
member.summary: Gets or sets a coordinate system grid to display on top of the .

remarks.para: The is a collection of horizontal and vertical lines which can be rendered over the top of a to help show the location of the current . Supported grids include Military Grid Reference System (), United States National Grid (), Universal Transverse Mercator (), and a grid of latitude and longitude lines (). You can configure these grids by toggling their visibility and modifying their default layout and appearance. A grid will not display in the until a or have been loaded.
The default value is null.

member.value: A coordinate system grid to display on top of the .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.GeoView.ImageOverlays
member.summary: Gets or sets a collection of overlays for displaying images in the view.

remarks.para: An image overlay collection cannot belong to more that one .
If the collection contains multiple image overlays, they display in the order in which they were added to the collection.
So the first overlay in the collection (at index 0) displays at the bottom and each one that follows displays on top of the previous ones.
If the also has a , it displays on top of the .

member.value: A collection of overlays for displaying images in the view.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.MapView
member.summary: A user interface control that displays two-dimensional (2D) geographic content defined by a .

remarks.para: The MapView is a container that holds a . A Map holds a collection of operational layers and . Layers are visual representations of geographic information and come in many forms. The types of layers that are displayed in a MapView are two dimensional in nature; meaning they are displayed in X/Y (a.k.a. Latitude/Longitude) coordinate space.
There is only one Map per MapView and it is set by the Property. Multiple Map objects can be created and swapped out dynamically as the application is running in the MapView.
It controls the area (extent) of the that is visible and supports user interactions such as pan and zoom. A map view also provides access to the underlying layer data in a map.
To display a , add a MapView control to your app and assign the map to it. This loads the map and its content, such as a and collection of operational layers, and displays this content on screen.

User interactions such as pan, zoom, rotate, and identify or selection are supported in the using touch, mouse, keyboard, or pen/pencil. If required, you can override keys, clicks, and gestures to provide a specific user experience.

The visible area () of the is defined by the visible extent the map. To determine the current visible area or the center point and scale of a , call . Make sure that any user-initiated or programmatic navigation is complete before getting the current by calling .

You can programmatically set the visible area by specifying a viewpoint. For example, sets the visible area to the extent of a provided geometry, and centers the map view at a given point. Any geometries passed to these methods are automatically projected to match the of the map view's , if required.

In an MVC architecture, the represents the View tier. The Model tier is represented by the object which can provide a collection of operational layers and a . You can only set one per , but you can swap the with another when the application is running. See the Map view discussion in the developer guide for more information.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.MapView.RuntimeCoreNet#GeneratedWrappers#ICoreCallback_MapView_GeometryEditorChanged#GeometryEditorChanged
member.summary: Called when this 's 'geometryEditor property is changed by RTC.
member.remarks: If a has been assigned to a and is later re-assigned to another , then it will automatically be removed from the initial . This event allows the SDK to be aware that the initial has lost its .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.MapView.RuntimeCoreNet#GeneratedWrappers#ICoreCallback_MapView_GeometryEditorPerformSnapHaptic#GeometryEditorPerformSnapHaptic(RuntimeCoreNet.GeneratedWrappers.CoreSnapState)
member.summary: Indicates that the snap candidate used by the associated with the map view changed.
member.remarks:
The snap candidate may change when the pointer is moved close to or away from an existing snappable geometry or geometry guide. When this change results in a different snap candidate, or the edit position becomes unsnapped, this event fires.
This occurs only when the map view has an associated geometry editor which is started, and the editor's ref@SnapSettings.isHapticFeedbackEnabled property is true.
This event can be used by SDKs implementing haptic feedback for geometry editor snapping where accessing the event from the map view is preferred.Alternatively, SDKs can listen to the public ref@GeometryEditor.snapChanged event if preferred - in this case SDKs should also check ref@SnapSettings.isHapticFeedbackEnabled, and depending on implementation may need to account for changes to the ref@MapView.geometryEditor in the property setter and also by listening to ref@MapView.geometryEditorChanged order to account for changes to ref@MapView.geometryEditor coming from RTC.
See ref@SnapState for general recommendations on which haptic feedback to use for responding to different types of snap.
RTC will call this from a background thread. It will only be called when the map view is resumed (ref@GeoView.isPaused is false).
member.param: Information about the snap that occurred during interactive editing.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.MapView.ScreenToLocation(System.Windows.Point)
member.summary: Converts screen coordinates relative to the upper-left corner of the map view to a location in map coordinates.
member.remarks: To call this method, assign a map to the map view, ensure that it is loaded and the draw status is .
member.param: The screen coordinate, in device-independent pixels (DIPs).
member.returns: A in map coordinates if this view is initialzied and has a loaded ; otherwise null.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.MapView.LocationToScreen(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Geometry.MapPoint)
member.summary: Converts a location in map coordinates to a screen coordinate relative to the upper-left corner of the map.
member.param: A location defined within the spatial reference of the map view.
member.returns: Screen location in this map control's local display coordinate system

remarks.para: The screen location returned is relative to the upper left corner of the map control. If you need a location relative to another visual element, use the TransformToVisual method to create a transformation between the map and another visual element.

The screen coordinates are in device-independent pixels (DIP) relative to the upper-left corner at position 0,0. The screen coordinates returned may lie outside the current map view bounds. For example, if a map of the world is zoomed in to the extent of Africa, a map location within South America would result in a screen coordinate that is outside the screens bounds.

If the is enabled on the map view, this method returns the closest screen location matching the specified map location. If the map coordinate is in the visible area the method will return that screen coordinate, otherwise it will return the screen coordinate from the frame closest to the visible area.
To call this method, assign a map to the map view, ensure that it is loaded and the draw status is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.MapView.MapScale
member.summary: Gets the scale of the .

remarks.para: You can observe changes to this property by listening to the event.

The scale represents the relationship between a distance in the (on the screen) and the corresponding distance on the ground. For example, a scale of 100,000 indicates that one centimeter on the display equates to one kilometer on the ground.
Users can interactively change the scale using the map view's zooming gestures. You can set the scale programmatically using methods that set the , such as .
The value is until the load status of the is and the draw status of the is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.MapView.MapRotation
member.summary: Gets the rotation angle of the in degrees from its north-south direction.

remarks.para: You can observe changes to this property by listening to the event.

If the map has been rotated in a clockwise direction the rotation value is negative. If it has been rotated in a counterclockwise direction the value is positive. For example, if the rotation value is -90 the top of the will display an eastern part of the .
Users can interactively rotate the map using the map view's keyboard, mouse or touch rotation gestures. You can rotate the map programmatically using methods that set the , such as or .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.MapView.UnitsPerPixel
member.summary: Gets the size of each device-independent pixel (DIP) in map units.
member.remarks:
Returns if the map isn't in a ready-state.
You can observe changes to this property by listening to the event.
The represents the spatial resolution of the . The value changes according to the (it decreases as the user zooms in, for example).
The value is until the load status of the is and the draw status of the is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.MapView.VisibleArea
member.summary: Gets the view's visible area.

remarks.para: The visible area represents the portion of the that is visible in the . When a new is assigned to a , the default value of the visible area is set from the .
Users can interactively navigate the map to change the visible area, or you can programmatically change this using methods that set the , such as .

The visible area polygon always contains one ring with four vertices, each representing a corner of the map. It is a and not an because the map may be rotated and each corner of the map may contain unique x-y coordinates. Note that the visible area excludes the portion of the map obscured by the map view's attribution bar. As a result, the edges and center of the visible area may not coincide with the bounds and center of the .
Returns null if the map isn't in a ready-state.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.MapView.WrapAroundMode
member.summary: Gets or sets whether continuous panning across the international date line is enabled.

remarks.para: Note that not all spatial references supports this mode, and setting it to true in those cases, this property will have no effect. You can check the property to determine if this MapView is wrapping.

By default, the attempts to wrap the across the international date line for a continuous panning user experience. The eastern and western hemispheres wrap to form a continuous map, giving the impression that the map is endless.
You can apply wraparound to a if certain conditions are met, as described in . To remove the wraparound behavior set the value to .

If wraparound is enabled, geometries returned from may have coordinates outside the domain of the spatial reference of the map. Before using such geometries to perform spatial queries, address finding, or as feature geometries in a geodatabase, normalize them to lie within the spatial reference domain using .
The default value is .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.MapView.LocationDisplay
member.summary: Gets the location display that manages and renders the device's current location on a using a data source, such as a GPS sensor.

remarks.para: The device location is displayed as a blue, round symbol that is automatically refreshed by regular updates from the . Although the uses the device's location data source by default, you can configure it to use data sources such as NMEA, route tracker, indoors, and simulated data sources. For more information see .

Use the enumerations to build navigation or compass style apps. For example, adopt to build an app that pans the so that the current location symbol is shown near the bottom of the screen and the is aligned with the direction of travel. You can also customize the symbols that display the device's location, its heading, the accuracy of the signal and the acquiring signal.

If the contains transparent areas, the may be visible within the and you may wish to define a appropriate to your map's symbology. If a has a , the is ignored.
The default color is gray with black grid lines.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.MapView.GeometryEditor
member.summary: Gets or sets the editor that allows users to interactively create and edit geometries by interacting with the view.

remarks.para: After assigning a to the , a being edited by the will be displayed using the context, orientation and spatial reference of the .

It may be useful to transfer a , with its partly edited , from one to another . For example, in a multi-view application, partway through editing, a user may want to see and continue editing the partially edited geometry in a using a different , rotation or (without changing the settings of their initial ).

A can only be connected to one at a time. If the same is assigned to a second without first detaching, then the previous will automatically have its property changed to null. The will project the to the of the new .
Starting or stopping editing using the is independent of the being attached to a .

If editing the has already been started (using ) then reassignment of the will automatically: - stop the editing, retain any changes, but undo can't be called on those changes - start the editing with the changed projected to the new .
There is no difference between calling before or after the is assigned to a . does not detach the from its current .

If the of the is changed (for example, by a different being connected to the ) then the will automatically take the same actions: - stop the editing, retain any changes, but undo can't be called on those changes - start the editing with the changed projected to the new .

member.value: The editor that allows users to interactively create and edit geometries by interacting with the view.

member.name: T:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView
member.summary: A user interface control that displays three-dimensional (3D) geographic content defined by a .

remarks.para: The SceneView is a container that holds a Scene. A Scene holds a collection of operational layers and . Layers are visual representations of geographic information and come in many forms. The types of layers that are displayed in a SceneView are three dimensional.

A is a user interface that displays layers and graphics in 3D. It uses a camera to control the visible area (extent) of the and supports user interactions such as pan, zoom, tilt, and rotate. A scene view also provides access to the underlying layer data in a scene.
To display a , add a control to your app and assign the scene to it. This loads the scene and its content, such as a , and collection of operational layers, and displays this content on screen.

User interactions such as pan, zoom, tilt, rotate, and identify or selection are supported in the using touch, mouse, keyboard, or pen/pencil. If required, you can override keys, clicks, and gestures to provide a specific user experience.

The visible area () of a is defined by the position and orientation of a . To determine the current visible area, call . Make sure that any user-initiated or programmatic navigation is complete before getting the current by calling .

You can programmatically set the visible area by specifying a viewpoint. For example, to zoom to the specified by a camera, pass the to the using one of its methods, such as . The has a camera controller () to manage the camera's movement and user interactions.

In an MVC architecture, the represents the View tier. The Model tier is represented by the object which can provide a collection of operational layers, a and a . You can only set one per , but you can swap the with another when the application is running. See the Scene view discussion in the developer guide for more information.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView.ScreenToBaseSurface(System.Windows.Point)
member.summary: Converts the specified screen coordinate, relative to the upper-left corner of the scene view, to a location on the base surface in map coordinates.
member.remarks: To call this method, assign a scene to the scene view, ensure that it is loaded and the draw status is .
member.param: The screen coordinate, in pixels. The coordinate of the top left corner of the screen is 0,0.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView.LocationToScreen(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Geometry.MapPoint)
member.summary: Converts a location in map coordinates to a point in screen coordinates relative to the upper-left corner of the scene view.
member.param: A location defined within the spatial reference of the scene view.
member.returns: Screen location in this map control's local display coordinate system

remarks.para: The screen coordinates are in device-independent pixels (DIP) relative to the upper-left corner of the scene view at position 0,0. If you need a location relative to another visual element, use the TransformToVisual method to create a transformation between the map and another visual element.
To call this method, assign a scene to the scene view, ensure that it is loaded and the draw status is .

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView.LocationToScreen(Esri.ArcGISRuntime.Geometry.MapPoint,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.LocationVisibility@)
member.summary: Converts a location in map coordinates to a screen coordinate relative to the upper-left corner of the scene.
member.param: The location in world coordinates to convert to screen coordinate.
The visibility of the point on the screen.
member.returns: Screen location in this scene view control's local display coordinate system.
member.remarks: The screen location returned is relative to the upper left corner of the scene view control. If you need a location relative to another visual element, use the TransformToVisual method to create a transformation between the map and another visual element.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView.ScreenToLocationAsync(System.Windows.Point)
member.summary: Asynchronously converts a screen coordinate relative to the upper-left corner of the scene view to a location in map coordinates.

remarks.para: This is a high performance calculation executed on the GPU using a triangular mesh. Note that elevation values are approximated, and as the distance between the camera and the surface increases, the precision of the elevation value decreases.
To call this method, assign a scene to the scene view, ensure that it is loaded and the draw status is .

This method checks for intersection between the input screen point and visible 3D features on the Scene. It returns a point with X, Y and Z values matching the intersection point. If the input point does not intersect a visible 3D feature, the Scene is used to retrieve the point values. If the input point does not intersect the Scene Surface or any visible 3D features a null value is returned.

This method differs from in that the latter only uses the Scene Surface and ignores visible 3D features. The algorithm is inherently slower than ScreenToBaseSurface and is therefore an async operation.

member.param: The screen coordinate, in pixels. The coordinate of the top left corner of the screen is 0,0.
member.returns: A on the base surface of the , if this view is initialized and has a loaded scene; otherwise null. This may also return null if the given is not on the base surface at all (for example, if the sky is drawn at those coordinates).

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView.SetFieldOfView(System.Double)
member.summary: Sets the horizontal field of view of the scene view in degrees.
member.param: The field of view on the scene view in degrees.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView.SetFieldOfView(System.Double,System.Double)
member.summary: Sets the field of view on the scene view in degrees and determines how much the vertical field of view is distorted.
member.remarks: A distortion factor of 1.0 is default. A distortion factor less than 1.0 will cause the visuals to be stretched taller in comparison to their width. A distortion factor greater than 1.0 will cause the visuals to be shrunk shorter in comparison to their width.
member.param: The field of view on the scene view in degrees.
The field of view vertical distortion ratio.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView.SetFieldOfView(System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single,Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.DeviceOrientation)
member.summary: Matches the field of view of the scene view to the field of view of a camera lens using the lens intrinsics characteristics.
member.param: The pixel focal length along the x-axis. The units are in pixels. xFocal and yFocal should be identical for square pixels.
The pixel focal length along the y-axis. The units are in pixels. xFocal and yFocal should be identical for square pixels.
The distance along the x-axis between the principal point and the top-left corner of the image frame. The units are in pixels.
The distance along the y-axis between the principal point and the top-left corner of the image frame. The units are in pixels.
The x value of the image size captured by the camera. The units are in pixels.
The y value of the image size captured by the camera. The units are in pixels.
Describes the orientation of the device.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView.FieldOfView
member.summary: Gets the horizontal field of view of the scene view in degrees.
member.remarks: The value of the field of view is influenced by the size and orientation of the device screen. A landscape orientation has a larger field of view value than a portrait orientation.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView.FieldOfViewDistortionRatio
member.summary: Gets how much the vertical field of view is distorted.
member.remarks: A distortion factor of 1.0 is default. A distortion factor less than 1.0 will cause the visuals to be stretched taller in comparison to their width. A distortion factor greater than 1.0 will cause the visuals to be shrunk shorter in comparison to their width.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView.CameraController
member.summary: Gets or sets the camera controller that manages the position, orientation, and movement of the camera for a .

remarks.para: The controller handles user-interaction with the and updates the . You can assign a with a specific navigation mode that is free roaming, following a moving target, fixed on a stationary target, or that supports AR transformation matrices. For more information, see .
The default camera is the .

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView.SunTime
member.summary: Gets or sets the position of the sun in the scene view based on a specific date and time.

remarks.para: The position of sunset, sunrise, and the night-time area of darkness are visible in the scene view as calculated from the value. These lighting effects are visible if you have set the modes of and .
The default value is Fri Sep 22 2000 12:00:00 GMT+0000.

member.name: P:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.Controls.SceneView.SpaceEffect
member.summary: Gets or sets the visual effect of outer space in the scene view. The modes are black with stars or transparent.
member.remarks: Setting the background to none will hide the starry sky effect and make the background color completely transparent. The atmosphere effect will remain unchanged. Default is . To create an augmented reality experience set this to to render through to the camera view behind it.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.DesktopDeviceInteractionHandler.IsWithinFuzzyTapThreshold(System.Double,System.Double,System.TimeSpan)
member.summary: This method is used to dynamically increase the tolerance for a tap event when user is clicking rapidly across the screen which may cause an unintentional drag when a tap was intended.
member.param: the horizontal distance the mouse has traveled in DIPs since the mouse down event.
the vertical distance the mouse has traveled in DIPs since the mouse down event.
the time elapsed since mouse down.
member.returns: true if the event should be considered a tap event and false if it should be a drag event.

member.name: M:Esri.ArcGISRuntime.UI.FlickCalculationHandler.GetFlickSpeed(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculates the weighted moving average of the mouse cursor over the up to last 10 mouse locations. Weight is based on the time of the events
member.param: The current time, as measured in the platform's motion event units

The temporal threshold that all events in the calculation set must be within to be considered a flick. When the oldest event in the set is older than this value, the flick speed will not be calculated.
member.returns: A point containing the weighted average in the X and Y directions if the events contained within the calculation set are determined to be a flick. Otherwise, default().
